commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5559962f78f5a63d5d5531634cc72cc23ad6cde5)
commands.review_summary:
The branch prunes stale unended restored runs even when `abortedLastRun` is true, skips stale runs during orphan recovery, updates subagent liveness docs, and changes the persistence regression test.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests -9, Docs 0. Total -1 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: on the PR branch, a stale active run with `abortedLastRun: true` enters the new `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` branch and is counted only as skipped, while the scheduler finalizes only `failedRuns`. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because contributor real behavior proof is still mock-only and maintainers must accept the stale session-state cutoff before any fixed version can merge.

target.title: fix(agents): apply stale-run liveness check to aborted subagent orphan recovery
target.body:
## Summary

Fix #90766: orphan recovery resurrects stale subagent sessions after gateway restart.

Two changes:
1. **`subagent-registry-helpers.ts`**: Remove the `abortedLastRun` exemption in `resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` so stale-unended runs are always pruned regardless of their abort status
2. **`subagent-orphan-recovery.ts`**: Add `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` check in `recoverOrphanedSubagentSessions` to skip runs older than the stale-run window (default 2h) before attempting recovery

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:**
Stale subagent runs (>2h) with `abortedLastRun: true` were previously exempt from stale-unended pruning during registry restore and orphan recovery, causing very old aborted child sessions to be resurrected after long gateway downtime. This fix removes the exemption so stale runs are always pruned.

**Real environment tested:**
Local dev machine, Node 22.19+, Linux x86_64, pnpm, openclaw repo @ `5559962f78`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-run-liveness.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > persists completed subagent timing into the child session entry
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > skips cleanup when cleanupHandled was persisted
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > maps legacy announce fields into cleanup state
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > returns isolated clones for unchanged persisted registry snapshots
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > reuses the persisted registry cache on hot internal read snapshots
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > returns empty maps for unchanged invalid persisted registry snapshots
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > normalizes persisted and newly registered session keys
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > retries cleanup announce after a failed announce
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > retries cleanup announce after announce flow rejects
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > keeps delete-mode runs retryable when announce is deferred
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > reconciles orphaned restored runs by pruning them from registry
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > reconciles stale unended restored runs that are not restart-recoverable
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > prunes stale unended restored runs with abortedLastRun instead of keeping them for recovery
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > removes attachments when pruning orphaned restored runs
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > prefers active runs and can resolve them from persisted registry snapshots
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > can resolve the newest child-session row even when an older stale row is still active
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > resume guard prunes orphan runs before announce retry
 ✓ subagent registry persistence > uses isolated temp state when OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR is unset in tests
 Tests  18 passed (18)
```

Key behavioral change: the test "prunes stale unended restored runs with abortedLastRun instead of keeping them for recovery" replaces the previous test that asserted stale aborted runs were KEPT. It validates:
- 3-hour-old run with `abortedLastRun: true` is created
- Registry restart occurs
- The run is pruned (removed from registry)
- `callGateway` is never called (no recovery attempted)

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.test.ts
 Tests  19 passed (19)
```

**Observed result after fix:**
- All 18 persistence tests pass, including the updated test that validates stale aborted runs are pruned instead of kept for recovery
- All 19 orphan-recovery tests pass: the stale-run check does not break normal recovery of fresh aborted sessions
- All liveness tests pass: the `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` contract remains unchanged

**What was not tested:**
Live gateway restart with multi-hour downtime and real subagent sessions. The stale-run window (2h default) makes end-to-end live testing impractical. The stale-unended run threshold is config-aware (respecting explicit `runTimeoutSeconds`) and the fix reuses the existing `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` helper that already has dedicated liveness tests.

Fixes: #90766

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: huangting-xy
